entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Important food security crop in semi-arid tropics

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Geographical region with warm temperatures and limited rainfall.

entities.name: DART
entities.description: Diversity Arrays Technology, a low-cost, high-throughput genotyping method.

entities.name: SSR
entities.description: Simple Sequence Repeat markers, a type of genetic marker.

entities.name: GENOTYPES
entities.description: The genetic constitution of an organism.

entities.name: INBREDS
entities.description: Genetically uniform lines derived from repeated selfing.

entities.name: RILS
entities.description: Recombinant Inbred Lines, a population derived from crossing two inbred lines and repeatedly selfing the progeny.

entities.name: H 77/833-2 9 PRLT 2/89-33
entities.description: Pearl millet cross used to create a mapping population.

entities.name: LINKAGE MAP
entities.description: A genetic map showing the relative positions of genetic markers.

entities.name: GENETIC MARKERS
entities.description: DNA sequences with known locations on a chromosome.

entities.name: GENOME COVERAGE
entities.description: The extent to which a genome is represented by genetic markers.

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE TRAITS
entities.description: Traits that vary continuously and are influenced by multiple genes.

entities.name: BACKGROUND SELECTION
entities.description: Selecting for the desired genome background during backcrossing.

entities.name: COMPARATIVE GENOMICS
entities.description: Comparing the genomes of different organisms.

entities.name: GENOME ORGANIZATION
entities.description: The arrangement of genes and other DNA sequences within a genome.

entities.name: DNA SEQUENCES
entities.description: The order of nucleotides in a DNA molecule.

relationships.weight: Pearl millet contributes to food security.
DArT is applied to pearl millet.
SSR is applied to pearl millet.
Inbreds are used to determine genotypes.
RILs are derived from the cross H 77/833-2 9 PRLT 2/89-33.
The linkage map is constructed using DArT markers.
The linkage map is constructed using SSR markers.
The linkage map improves genome coverage.
The linkage map is used for genetic analyses of quantitative traits.
DArT is used for background selection.
DArT is used for comparative genomics.
DArT is used for genome organization studies.
Genome organization studies require DNA sequences.
